name: Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. There are as many versions of vegetable soup in Italy as there are cooks.
2. Pancetta is the same cut of pork as bacon but is cured with salt and not smoked. It comes rolled up like a large salami and is widely used in Italian cooking, especially in Emilia-Romagna. If available, buy a large quantity and freeze it. You can substitute domestic bacon for pancetta; blanch it in boiling water for two to three minutes to reduce the smoky flavor.
3. Prepare chicken broth (see my previously posted recipe).
4. Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
5. Add chopped parsley and minced garlic, then sauté until fragrant.
6. Before the garlic changes color, add pancetta; cook until lightly browned.
7. Stir in shredded cabbage and cover; cook for 1 to 2 minutes.
8. Add the remaining vegetables (onion, carrots, celery, potato, zucchini, tomato) and cover; cook about 5 minutes.
9. Add chicken broth and water (if using), then prosciutto rind or ham shank. Cover and reduce heat.
10. Simmer for 40 to 50 minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
11. Remove half the cooked vegetables with a slotted spoon, place them in a blender or food processor, and process until smooth. Return these blended vegetables to the saucepan.
12. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
13. Serve hot, topped with grated Parmesan cheese. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
Variation:
Toast about 20 thick slices of Italian bread. Place two slices in each soup bowl, sprinkle generously with Parmesan cheese, ladle soup into bowls, and serve with additional Parmesan cheese on top.
